mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity values and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market slack, which depresses aggregate wage income and consumption expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in real retail sales and an increase in consumer prices signals stagflationary demand destruction that compresses corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month change negative and CPIAUCSL 1-month change positive
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling RSAFS and rising CPIAUCSL is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in housing starts signal amplified interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that reduces construction and durable goods activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month declines in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and corporate revenue growth that expands earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month jump in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 index points signals an upside inflation surprise that forces a monetary policy repricing and raises real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.5 points in a month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and increases in the unemployment rate confirm a labor market contraction that reduces aggregate income and consumption, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month and UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In tight labor markets where unemployment is below 4.0%, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals demand-driven earnings expansion without slack-induced cost pressures, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
